diffs.diff:
--- a/train.py
+++ b/train.py
@@ -37,11 +37,34 @@
     return torch.mean((clean_mag - enhanced_mag)**2)

 

 

+def compute_perceptual_loss(clean, enhanced):

+    """Compute a perceptual loss based on magnitude differences in log scale."""

+    device = clean.device

+    

+    # Compute STFT

+    n_fft = 512

+    hop_length = 128

+    clean_stft = torch.stft(clean, n_fft=n_fft, hop_length=hop_length, 

+                           win_length=n_fft, window=torch.hann_window(n_fft).to(device),

+                           return_complex=True)

+    enhanced_stft = torch.stft(enhanced, n_fft=n_fft, hop_length=hop_length,

+                              win_length=n_fft, window=torch.hann_window(n_fft).to(device),

+                              return_complex=True)

+    

+    # Compute magnitude spectrograms in log scale

+    eps = 1e-8

+    clean_log_mag = torch.log(torch.abs(clean_stft) + eps)

+    enhanced_log_mag = torch.log(torch.abs(enhanced_stft) + eps)

+    

+    # Return L1 loss in log magnitude domain

+    return torch.mean(torch.abs(clean_log_mag - enhanced_log_mag))

+

+

 def main():

     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Train speech enhancement model')

     parser.add_argument('--data_dir', type=str, default='/data', help='Path to data directory')

     parser.add_argument('--batch_size', type=int, default=8, help='Batch size')

-    parser.add_argument('--num_epochs', type=int, default=10, help='Number of epochs')

+    parser.add_argument('--num_epochs', type=int, default=5, help='Number of epochs')

     parser.add_argument('--learning_rate', type=float, default=1e-3, help='Learning rate')

     parser.add_argument('--max_len', type=int, default=16384, help='Max length of audio segments')

     args = parser.parse_args()

     # Training loop

     for epoch in range(args.num_epochs):

@@ -84,10 +108,13 @@

             enhanced = model(noisy)

-            total_loss = time_domain_loss + 0.5 * freq_domain_loss  # Weight the frequency domain loss
